concerns.analysis: The source document contains the 'North Carolina Marine Fisheries' seal/logo on the top left of the first page. In the rendered HTML output, this image is missing entirely as an element, and instead appears as a text placeholder within a paragraph tag. Per the instructions, a missing agency logo on the first page of a government document constitutes a critical failure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries on page 1
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries celebrating 200 years of management and conservation (1822-2022)]

concerns.analysis: The source document contains lists that use Roman numerals (I, II, III) and uppercase letters (A, B, C). However, the output JSON converts these as generic 'ordered' lists without specifying the list type (e.g., type='a' or type='I'), and instead bakes the markers ('A.', 'B.', 'I.') directly into the list item text strings. This causes a numbering type mismatch where the default numbers (1, 2, 3) will render alongside the hardcoded text letters, violating the instruction that ordered lists with
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A. This proclamation is issued under the authority of...
concerns.output_quote: "type": "list", "list_type": "ordered", "items": [ { "text": "A. This proclamation is issued under the authority of..."

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 3 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=3, examples=['https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'http://www.ncmarinefisheries.net/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

The source PDF heavily uses bolding and underlining for emphasis (e.g., the entirety of the list items under Section II, and the inline bold labels for Section III list items). The output completely loses this visual emphasis, stripping both the bold markdown and any indication of the underline structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: It is unlawful for persons participating in a commercial fishing operation employing fish pots, traps, and/or hook and line gear, regardless of the number of people involved, to possess or land more than 2,000 pounds of black sea bass per trip. (Fully bolded and underlined in the PDF)
concerns.output_quote: A. It is unlawful for persons participating in a commercial fishing operation employing fish pots, traps, and/or hook and line gear, regardless of the number of people involved, to possess or land more than 2,000 pounds of black sea bass per trip.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries

concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
